political.s: Via parent company T-Mobile US: FEC: $378K in executive political donations; partisan lean split across 15 executive donors.

labor.s: Via parent company T-Mobile US: 104 federal penalties totaling $1.9B since 2000 (Violation Tracker).

environment.s: Via parent company T-Mobile US: CDP Climate Change: B (2024) — management-band disclosure; discloses emissions data but limited target ambition.

dei.s: Via parent company T-Mobile US: HRC Corporate Equality Index: 100/100 — Equality 100 Leader (2025). Top-rated LGBTQ+ workplace policies, benefits, and culture. HRC Corporate Equality Index 100/100 (2025) — top-tier LGBTQ+ workplace equality rating.

privacy.s: Via parent company T-Mobile US: 1 publicly-disclosed data breach per Have I Been Pwned (2021) — 47M accounts compromised. Most recent: T-Mobile (2021).

execPay.s: Via parent company T-Mobile US: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 5:1 (median employee pay $1,500,000) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2026).

wiki.extract: Metro by T-Mobile, formerly known as MetroPCS, is an American prepaid wireless service provider and brand owned by T-Mobile US. It previously operated the fifth largest mobile telecommunications network in the United States using code-division multiple access (CDMA). In 2013, the carrier engaged in a reverse merger with T-Mobile US; post-merger, its services were merged under T-Mobile's UMTS and LTE network. Metro by T-Mobile competes primarily against AT&T's Cricket Wireless and Verizon's Total Wireless as part of the wireless service provider brands. Metro by T-Mobile had twenty million subs

health.s: Via parent company T-Mobile US: CPUC enforcement: $4.4M citation for service-quality / 911 / Lifeline. CPUC fined T-Mobile $4.4M for violations of California LifeLine program rules including improper de-enrollment of eligible low-income subscribers and reimbursement-fund overclaims.
